definitions: 大众传媒（指报纸、电视、广播等广泛传播信息的媒介）

examples.text: Secondly, environmental groups need to be noticed by the mass media. They also need to keep the money rolling in. Understandably, perhaps, they sometimes overstate their arguments. In 1997, for example, the World Wide Fund for Nature issued a press release entitled: 'Two thirds of the world's forests lost forever'. The truth turns out to be nearer 20%.
examples.explanation: 其次，环保组织需要被大众传媒关注。他们还需要持续获得资金支持。可以理解的是，他们有时会夸大论点。例如1997年世界自然基金会发布的新闻稿称：'全球三分之二森林永久消失'，实际数据却接近20%。
